title: eryngium bourgatii subsp. heldreichii
intro: Eryngium bourgatii subsp. heldreichii, also known as the Heldreich's eryngium or sea holly, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and blue flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its ability to reduce pain, swelling, and oxidative stress. The health benefits associated with Eryngium bourgatii subsp. heldreichii include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
